seeds.note: historical v1.3-promoted primary seed (judge_rho 0.8274 vs relabel_v1 val labels on the old base) -- run first per plan so a single-seed result exists even if a later seed is delayed

sampler_path_resolution: confirmed for all 3 seeds via the same logic capture_judge_responses_tinker.py._resolve_tinker_path uses: manifest['promoted'] names a checkpoints[] entry whose 'name' matches, and that entry's 'sampler_path' is the resolved tinker:// URI -- verified directly against each committed manifest, not merely asserted
note: JUDGE-02: 3-seed relabel-SFT (seeds 1/0/2) on the new base (Qwen/Qwen3.6-35B-A3B), reusing the v1.3 human-relabeled targets verbatim (label_source, unchanged from the old-base v1.3 run per V4-RERUN-ROADMAP discretion item 2 -- no re-labeling, no regeneration). All three ran concurrently as independent remote Tinker jobs, each producing 3 per-epoch persistent (ttl=None) sampler checkpoints; none pruned. No merge or serve performed here -- that is JUDGE-03 (21-06), which capture-evals each seed's promoted checkpoint against the pre-registered rho targets (>0.85 single / >0.87 ensemble) and decides on ensembling.
